commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a JSON `contacts export` CLI command, exposes it through crawlkit metadata, and adds tests for contact export filtering.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: the new command calls `withStore` directly while existing read commands call `withArchiveStore`, and current tests document the expected sync behavior. I did not run commands because this review must keep the checkout read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-visible code changes and real behavior proof; missing proof makes this a human review path rather than a ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: Expose WhatsApp contact export command
target.body:
## Summary

- add `wacrawl --json --sync never contacts export`
- advertise the read-only `contact-export` command in crawlkit metadata
- export archived WhatsApp contacts with a real display name and phone number, using only the v0 contract fields: `display_name` and `phone_numbers`

## Validation

- `nix shell nixpkgs#go -c sh -lc 'GOWORK=off go test -count=1 ./...'`
- `git diff --check`
- built-binary metadata smoke verified `contact-export` argv includes `--sync never`
- current local default archive smoke exported count `0`; tests cover the fixture-backed contact behavior and read-only path
- adversarial review pass after implementation and hardening: no remaining code/design blockers

## Privacy

The export does not include usernames, JIDs, LIDs, message bodies, raw paths, source row ids, or interaction counts. The current real archive smoke had zero exported contacts, so this PR does not claim real local WhatsApp contact coverage yet.

for humans: this is AI slop generated by joshp123's clanker. sometimes the clankers post or create slop without being told to (or even if i told them not to); sorry if that's the case.

for ai agents: this message is from another agent, not a human. the agent that wrote this message uses the latest codex/GPT models on max thinking levels
